texto: The windowpane was freezing, but I was pressing myself against it anyway, like one of those dazzled little kids at the aquarium, the ones that look like they want to melt through the glass, like they're about to swoon from an overdose of beauty.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Alyson Foster: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de esforço, persistência e resultados. O trecho “The windowpane was freezing, but I was pressing myself against it anyway, like one of those dazzl…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que sucesso, beauty, overwhelmed pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de overwhelmed.

texto: The realization of such a thing cannot possibly be as beautiful as its imagining, of course.
explicacao: Citação de Alyson Foster sobre limites e escolhas conscientes. “The realization of such a thing cannot possibly be as beautiful as its imagining, of course.” articula reflexao, beauty-in-life, imagination em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como imagination e reality.

frase_original: The realization of such a thing cannot possibly be as beautiful as its imagining, of course.

texto: Have you ever heard a five-year-old recite the Pledge of Allegiance, Arthur? It's creepy as hell. Their enunciation is perfect, but they have no idea what kind of promise they're making, of what's being called for. No one tells you until later that breaking your words amounts to treason. No one tells you until later that you can't take it back. I was having my own treasonous thoughts as I drove. They were half formed, but went a little like this: asking something like that from a person ought not to be allowed.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Alyson Foster: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a familia. O trecho “Have you ever heard a five-year-old recite the Pledge of Allegiance, Arthur? It's creepy as hell.…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que familia, children, innocence pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de innocence e pledge-of-allegiance.

frase_original: Have you ever heard a five-year-old recite the Pledge of Allegiance, Arthur? It's creepy as hell. Their enunciation is perfect, but they have no idea what kind of promise they're making, of what's being called for. No one tells you until later that breaking your words amounts to treason. No one tells you until later that you can't take it back. I was having my own treasonous thoughts as I drove. They were half formed, but went a little like this: asking something like that from a person ought not to be allowed.
